title: Oil Supply Shock Spike
desc: Iran war premium driving WTI to 52W highs. RSI overbought but event-driven momentum intact. Near-term continuation thesis with high conviction.
strategy: Bull call spreads on energy sector / long calls on crude

commodities.name: WTI Crude Futures — Long Call
commodities.rationale: Pure directional leverage on Iran escalation continuation; exit immediately if Hormuz diplomatic headline emerges.

commodities.name: Brent Crude Futures — Bull Call Spread
commodities.rationale: Defined-risk play on Brent catching up to WTI premium; spread limits cost in already overbought market.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Bear Put Spread
fixed_income.rationale: Oil shock is inflationary; rising energy prices force Fed to stay hawkish, pressuring long duration bonds lower.

fixed_income.name: TIPS ETF — Long
fixed_income.rationale: TIPS explicitly compensate for CPI increases driven by energy spike; inflation breakevens should widen as oil stays elevated.

currencies.name: Canadian Dollar — Long vs USD
currencies.rationale: Canada is a net oil exporter; CAD historically rallies 0.6–0.8% per 10% WTI increase, offering a leveraged FX play on the oil thesis.

currencies.name: Norwegian Krone — Long vs EUR
currencies.rationale: Norway's sovereign wealth fund and petro-krone status make NOK one of the most direct FX expressions of oil strength in developed markets.

equities.name: XLE Energy ETF — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: Sector-level exposure captures the oil spike across the energy complex; selling the $66 call finances the trade — energy rarely runs further in 60 days without a catalyst shift.

equities.name: Chevron — Long Calls
equities.rationale: CVX is the highest-quality oil major with integrated downstream; benefits from oil spike while dividend floor provides earnings support even if oil retreats.

title: Gold Safe Haven Bid
desc: Geopolitical flare-ups and inflation driving safe haven flows. Gold above SMA200 with tactical entries on pullbacks toward $4,500.
strategy: Long calls / bull call spreads on gold & gold proxies

commodities.name: Gold Futures — Long Call
commodities.rationale: Near-the-money call captures upside on the next geopolitical catalyst; defined risk with leverage on a thesis with high conviction and multiple drivers.

commodities.name: Silver Futures — Bull Call Spread
commodities.rationale: Silver offers 2–3x leverage to gold moves with added industrial demand from solar; spread caps risk given silver's volatility vs gold.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Long Calls
fixed_income.rationale: Flight-to-safety flows that drive gold simultaneously bid US Treasuries; TLT calls capture the correlated safe-haven rally in rates.

fixed_income.name: TIPS ETF — Long Position
fixed_income.rationale: TIPS provide inflation protection while gold provides crisis protection; holding both hedges both inflation and deflation tail scenarios simultaneously.

currencies.name: Swiss Franc — Long vs USD
currencies.rationale: CHF is the quintessential safe-haven currency — negative-yielding in normal times but historically appreciates 3–5% in major risk-off episodes alongside gold.

currencies.name: Japanese Yen — Long vs USD
currencies.rationale: JPY carries negative beta to global risk appetite; unwinding of carry trades during stress events pushes JPY 5–8% in short periods — natural gold hedge complement.

equities.name: GDX Gold Miners ETF — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: GDX provides 2–3x operating leverage to gold prices; already up 142% in 1Y vs gold's 75% — structural bid continues as margins expand with higher gold prices.

equities.name: Barrick Gold — Long Calls
equities.rationale: Barrick is the highest-quality gold major with lowest all-in sustaining costs; captures leveraged upside to gold with lower volatility than GDX basket.

title: US Vol Premium Harvest
desc: VIX up 51% in 6M — option premiums richly priced. When VIX spikes above 25 and mean-reverts, short premium strategies become high-probability.
strategy: Iron condors / short strangles on VIX mean reversion

commodities.name: GLD Gold ETF — Iron Condor
commodities.entry: Sell $440/$460 call spread + Sell $400/$380 put spread
commodities.cost: Net credit ~$3.50 | max loss $16.50 either side
commodities.rationale: Gold's IV is elevated post-geopolitical spike; selling the wings captures premium while defining maximum loss — gold rarely moves 7% in 30 days without a new catalyst.

commodities.name: USO Oil ETF — Short Strangle
commodities.rationale: WTI's realized vs implied vol gap creates theta decay opportunity; manage at 50% profit or close before expiry if directional move materializes.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Iron Condor
fixed_income.entry: Sell $90/$93 call spread + Sell $83/$80 put spread
fixed_income.cost: Net credit ~$1.80 | max loss $1.20 either side
fixed_income.rationale: TLT rate vol is elevated but range-bound; iron condor profits if TLT stays between $80–93, which covers a 45bps move in 10Y yields in either direction.

fixed_income.name: AGG Bond ETF — Covered Call
fixed_income.entry: Sell covered call on existing AGG position
fixed_income.rationale: AGG moves in a narrow range; covered calls generate 0.6–0.8% monthly income on bond holdings while vol premium is elevated.

currencies.name: EUR/USD — Iron Condor
currencies.entry: Sell 25-delta call + Sell 25-delta put on EUR/USD options
currencies.rationale: EUR/USD realized vol has compressed despite elevated IV; mean-reversion in FX vol is faster than equities — 30-day window captures most of the decay.

currencies.name: UUP Dollar ETF — Short Strangle
currencies.rationale: UUP premiums elevated from geopolitical dollar demand; theta decay accelerates in final 14 days — close at 50% profit.

equities.name: SPY S&P 500 ETF — Iron Condor
equities.rationale: SPY is the most liquid options market; selling at 25-delta captures 70%+ probability of profit while defining risk to a manageable level in elevated VIX environment.

equities.name: QQQ Nasdaq 100 ETF — Short Strangle
equities.rationale: QQQ's higher vol vs SPY makes premium richer; manage at 50% max profit — do not hold through earnings season or geopolitical escalation events.

title: Dollar Strength Momentum
desc: DXY above all 3 MAs in strong uptrend. Fed higher-for-longer and risk-off flows supporting dollar. Near-term continuation.
strategy: Bull call spreads on dollar strength proxies

commodities.name: Gold Futures — Bear Put Spread
commodities.rationale: Dollar strength is inversely correlated with gold; DXY rallying above 102 has historically correlated with gold pullbacks of 5–8%.

commodities.name: Silver Futures — Bear Puts
commodities.rationale: Silver is more dollar-sensitive than gold; a 2% dollar rally typically causes 4–6% silver decline, amplifying the dollar thesis with higher-beta exposure.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Bear Puts
fixed_income.rationale: Dollar strength = higher yields = lower bond prices; TLT puts capture the rate component of the dollar thesis with defined risk.

fixed_income.name: BIL Short-Term Treasury ETF — Long
fixed_income.rationale: BIL yields 5%+ at the short end; a dollar-positive, higher-for-longer environment means front-end rates stay elevated — park cash in BIL instead of cash equivalents.

currencies.name: UUP US Dollar ETF — Long Calls
currencies.rationale: UUP is the most direct dollar-long vehicle in equity markets; call options provide leveraged upside with defined risk on the dollar momentum thesis.

currencies.name: EUR/USD — Bear Put Spread
currencies.rationale: EUR is the largest DXY component; ECB likely to cut before Fed given weaker EU growth — this divergence trade has been reliable in prior dollar bull cycles.

equities.name: EEM Emerging Market ETF — Bear Puts
equities.rationale: EM equities and currencies are the primary losers in dollar bull cycles; higher USD funding costs compress EM growth, margins, and capital flows simultaneously.

equities.name: EWJ Japan ETF — Long Calls
equities.rationale: Japanese equities benefit from yen weakness caused by dollar strength; Nikkei companies are primarily exporters — weak JPY directly boosts earnings in yen terms.

title: Gold & Silver Debasement Cycle
desc: Global sovereign debt expansion and currency debasement driving multi-year precious metals repricing. Silver adds industrial demand from solar and EVs.
strategy: Calendar spreads to roll long exposure, covered calls for premium income

commodities.name: Gold Futures — Calendar Spread
commodities.rationale: Calendar spreads allow persistent long exposure without option premium decay; rolling quarterly maintains the structural long while harvesting contango/backwardation.

commodities.name: Silver Futures — Long Call
commodities.rationale: Silver's supply deficit is structural; 6-month calls capture the thesis without week-to-week volatility, with time for the monetary + industrial demand to compound.

fixed_income.name: TIPS ETF — Long vs TLT Short
fixed_income.entry: Long TIP / Short TLT as pairs trade
fixed_income.cost: Minimal net capital if equal notional
fixed_income.rationale: The debasement cycle implies persistent inflation above expectations; TIPS breakevens should widen, making TIPS outperform nominal bonds — this spread trade captures exactly that.

fixed_income.name: I-Bond / Short TLT — Real Rate Hedge
fixed_income.rationale: Structural gold bull markets historically occur when long-end yields are rising; TLT puts hedge the rate component of the debasement thesis while gold positions provide the upside.

currencies.name: Swiss Franc — Long vs Multiple
currencies.entry: Long CHF against basket of EUR, USD
currencies.rationale: CHF is backed by the SNB's gold reserves and maintained as a hard currency sanctuary; in prolonged debasement cycles, CHF outperforms all fiat currencies.

currencies.name: Euro — Short vs Commodity Currencies
currencies.rationale: Euro area faces debasement risks without commodity backing; AUD benefits from gold/silver/copper exports — this cross captures the debasement cycle most precisely.

equities.name: GDX Gold Miners ETF — Calendar Spread
equities.rationale: GDX calendar spreads capture structural gold appreciation while selling near-term vol to reduce cost; miners' operating leverage means GDX outperforms gold in sustained bull markets.

equities.name: PAAS Pan American Silver — Long Calls
equities.rationale: PAAS offers pure silver operating leverage with gold production as a hedge; at current silver prices, PAAS generates exceptional cash flow — call options provide leveraged structural exposure.

title: AI Infrastructure Supercycle
desc: Generative AI driving unprecedented capex in semiconductors, data centers, and power. Multi-year compounding demand for compute, copper, and electricity.
strategy: Bull call spreads on tech / semis, roll quarterly positions

commodities.name: Copper Futures — Bull Call Spread
commodities.rationale: AI data center copper demand is quantifiable and growing; supply response takes 5–7 years from discovery to production — structural deficit is locked in, making directional calls appropriate.

commodities.name: UNG Natural Gas ETF — Tactical Long
commodities.entry: Buy at market with stop at -15%
commodities.rationale: AI data centers are the fastest-growing electricity consumers; gas peaker plants will supply a significant share of that power — nat gas demand curve is inflecting despite current oversupply.

fixed_income.name: Investment Grade Tech Bonds — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy IG corporate bond ETF with tech overweight
fixed_income.rationale: Investment grade tech bonds (MSFT, GOOGL, META) yield 5%+ while offering credit quality near sovereign level; AI revenue diversifies the balance sheets supporting these bonds.

fixed_income.name: Convertible Bond ETF — Long
fixed_income.rationale: Convertible bonds in AI-adjacent companies capture equity upside through conversion optionality while providing bond-floor downside protection — asymmetric AI infrastructure bet.

currencies.name: US Dollar — Structural Long
currencies.rationale: AI infrastructure investment creates massive USD demand — hyperscalers pay for semiconductors (TSMC), construction, and hardware in dollars; sustained capex = sustained dollar demand.

currencies.name: Taiwan Dollar — Long vs JPY
currencies.rationale: TWD benefits from TSMC's central role in AI chip production — every AI chip order is a TWD demand signal; JPY is weakening from BOJ policy, making this cross compelling.

equities.name: QQQ Nasdaq 100 — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: AI monetization is visible in earnings — Microsoft, Google, Meta all beat on AI revenue quarterly; the structural compounding means QQQ bull spreads have high probability of reaching the short strike.

equities.name: SMH Semiconductor ETF — Long Calls
equities.rationale: SMH contains the 25 largest semiconductor companies; AI demand drives NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, TSMC simultaneously — sector-level exposure diversifies single-name risk while capturing the structural wave.

title: Copper & Silver Commodity Cycle
desc: AI data centers, EV transition, and grid modernization creating decade-long demand surge. Supply cannot scale fast enough.
strategy: Long calls / bull spreads on metals, roll quarterly

commodities.name: Copper Futures — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: Copper supply cannot respond to demand surges; mine-to-market lead time is 10–15 years. Rolling calls on a quarterly basis maintains the structural long without unlimited futures risk.

commodities.name: Silver Futures — Bull Call Spread
commodities.rationale: Silver's industrial deficit is accelerating; the $75–95 range captures a structural move from current levels with defined risk. Sell the $95 strike — silver rarely moves 30% in 6 months without a fundamental catalyst.

fixed_income.name: EM Infrastructure Bonds — Long
fixed_income.rationale: Copper and silver producing nations (Chile, Peru, Mexico) issue EM bonds that benefit from commodity cycle; rising metal prices improve fiscal positions, compressing sovereign spreads.

fixed_income.name: Copper Producer Bonds — Long
fixed_income.rationale: FCX investment grade bonds offer equity-like upside via credit tightening as copper prices rise; FCX's balance sheet strengthens with every $0.10 copper increase — direct commodity thesis expression in fixed income.

currencies.name: Chilean Peso — Long vs USD (via EM proxy)
currencies.entry: Long CLP via EM FX basket or copper-linked EM ETF
currencies.rationale: Chile produces 27% of global copper; CLP correlates ~0.75 with copper prices — a structural copper rally implies CLP appreciation from extreme undervaluation vs fundamentals.

currencies.name: Australian Dollar — Long vs JPY
currencies.rationale: Australia is the world's second largest copper and silver producer; AUD is the highest-beta G10 expression of a commodity bull cycle, particularly vs the weakening JPY carry trade.

equities.name: COPX Copper Miners ETF — Long Calls
equities.rationale: COPX provides diversified exposure to copper miners globally; operating leverage means a 20% copper price increase translates to 40–60% earnings growth for producers — ETF format reduces single-mine risk.

equities.name: Freeport-McMoRan — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: FCX is the largest public copper producer globally; at $5.50 copper they generate exceptional free cash flow — this bull spread captures the move to fair value as copper's structural deficit reprices the equity.

title: Utilities & AI Power Demand
desc: AI data centers driving massive power demand. Utilities combine defensive characteristics with structural growth tailwind. XLU above all MAs.
strategy: Covered calls on utility positions for income

commodities.name: Natural Gas Futures — Tactical Long Calls
commodities.rationale: Gas-fired peakers supply AI data center power during grid stress periods; long-term structural demand from data center load growth supports gas floor pricing above $3.00 despite near-term oversupply.

commodities.name: UNG Natural Gas ETF — Bull Call Spread
commodities.rationale: Defined-risk natural gas play capturing the utility power demand thesis without unlimited futures exposure; UNG tracks front-month gas, which benefits first from demand inflection.

fixed_income.name: Utility Sector Bonds — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy investment grade utility bonds (NextEra, Duke, Southern)
fixed_income.rationale: AI PPA contracts provide 10–20 year revenue visibility — upgrading utilities' credit profiles; bonds yield 5%+ while credit quality is improving, creating the best risk/reward in IG bonds.

fixed_income.name: XLU Utilities ETF — Covered Call
fixed_income.rationale: XLU above all MAs in strong uptrend; covered calls generate 1–1.3% monthly income on top of the 3.2% dividend yield — total annual return potential of 15%+ in a range-bound scenario.

currencies.name: US Dollar — Neutral to Long
currencies.rationale: AI infrastructure buildout is US-centric; power plant construction, grid upgrades, and data center development are dollar-denominated — net inflow of capital to US utility sector supports dollar.

currencies.name: CAD — Tactical Long (Energy-Utility Link)
currencies.rationale: Canadian utilities and pipeline companies benefit from AI data center power demand spilling into cross-border energy infrastructure; CAD serves as a proxy for North American energy infrastructure.

equities.name: XLU Utilities ETF — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: XLU in confirmed uptrend with AI power demand tailwind; the $47–52 range captures a 12% move over 90 days as hyperscaler PPA announcements continue to re-rate the sector.

equities.name: NextEra Energy — Long Calls
equities.rationale: NEE is the world's largest renewable energy producer and AI power beneficiary; contracted for 10+ GW of data center power across Florida and Texas — highest-quality way to own the AI utility theme.

title: Gold Miners Amplified Play
desc: GDX offers 2–3x operating leverage to gold prices. Up 142% in 1Y vs gold's 75%. Structural gold bid continues.
strategy: Calendar spreads on GDX, covered calls on rallies

commodities.name: Gold Futures — Long for Underlying Exposure
commodities.entry: Long 1 micro gold contract per $5,000 portfolio
commodities.rationale: Holding physical gold exposure alongside miner equities provides the underlying bid that drives miner valuations; if miners underperform, the gold position provides direct upside.

commodities.name: PSLV Physical Silver Trust — Long
commodities.rationale: PSLV holds physical silver with no counterparty risk; silver's 134% 1Y move reflects genuine physical tightness — trust premium expansion adds an additional return layer on top of price appreciation.

fixed_income.name: Gold Miner Bonds — Long (Barrick/Newmont)
fixed_income.entry: Buy Newmont or Barrick investment grade bonds
fixed_income.rationale: Gold miner IG bonds yield 5%+ while credit quality is improving as gold prices rise; Newmont's AISC of $1,200–1,400/oz means at $4,651 gold, free cash flow is exceptional — bond risk is minimal.

fixed_income.name: TLT — Long Calls (Correlated Trade)
fixed_income.rationale: Gold and Treasuries are correlated in flight-to-safety episodes; when the debasement thesis is in full force, both gold miners and long bonds benefit — TLT calls hedge the macro macro scenario that accelerates gold.

currencies.name: Australian Dollar — Long vs USD
currencies.rationale: Australia is the world's second largest gold producer; AUD has 0.65+ correlation with gold — a gold bull market is effectively an implicit AUD long with currency diversification.

currencies.name: South African Rand — Selective Long
currencies.rationale: South Africa hosts the world's deepest gold deposits and major miners (AngloGold); ZAR is the highest-beta DM/EM currency to gold price moves, though political risk must be sized accordingly.

equities.name: GDX Gold Miners ETF — Calendar Spread
equities.rationale: Calendar spread captures structural gold appreciation while selling monthly premium; as short call expires worthless each month, total cost basis decreases — optimal structure for a 12-month thesis.

equities.name: Newmont Mining — Long Calls
equities.rationale: NEM is the world's largest gold producer with the lowest all-in sustaining costs; at $4,651 gold, NEM generates $2,000+/oz margin — the stock significantly undervalues the earnings power at current gold prices.

title: US Equity Near-Term Weakness
desc: SPX and QQQ both below SMA50 and SMA200. MACD negative, macro headwinds from rate uncertainty. Tactical short on oversold bounces.
strategy: Bear put spreads on index proxies, near-dated expiry

commodities.name: GLD Gold ETF — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: Equity weakness drives safe-haven flows to gold; historically GLD outperforms during S&P 500 corrections of 5–10% by 3–5 percentage points as risk-off rotation accelerates.

commodities.name: VIX Volatility Index — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: VIX tends to spike 5–10 points in S&P corrections; these calls provide convex protection — cheap insurance that pays significantly if the correction accelerates into a more disorderly selloff.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Long Calls
fixed_income.rationale: Flight-to-safety flows drive concurrent equity selloffs and bond rallies; TLT calls provide a positive-correlation hedge to the equity short — if equities fall, TLT typically rallies 2–4%.

fixed_income.name: IEI 3-7Y Treasury ETF — Long
fixed_income.rationale: Intermediate Treasuries are the safest risk-off parking spot; less rate-sensitive than TLT but more upside than short-term bills when equity stress triggers Fed rate cut expectations.

currencies.name: Japanese Yen — Long vs USD
currencies.rationale: JPY is the global risk-off safe haven; when US equities fall more than 3%, USD/JPY typically falls 2–4% within 2 weeks as carry trades unwind and institutional hedging flows to JPY.

currencies.name: Swiss Franc — Long vs EUR
currencies.rationale: CHF is the alternative G10 safe haven; EUR/CHF has tight range during normal markets but gaps 1–2% during European-driven stress events — defined-risk call captures the asymmetric protection.

equities.name: SPY S&P 500 ETF — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: The $620 target captures a 5% correction from current levels — historically typical for market consolidations when price is below both the SMA50 and SMA200 simultaneously.

equities.name: IWM Russell 2000 ETF — Bear Puts
equities.rationale: Small caps have higher debt-to-EBITDA and floating-rate exposure; IWM underperforms SPY in rate stress environments by an additional 2–3% — higher beta to the downside thesis.

title: Natural Gas Breakdown
desc: NG down 62% from 52W high, below all MAs, MACD deeply negative. Oversupply persists. Rallies are distribution.
strategy: Bear put spreads / long puts on natural gas

commodities.name: NG=F Natural Gas Futures — Bear Put Spread
commodities.rationale: NG at $2.80 with all technical indicators pointing lower; the $2.30 target captures the next major support level — EIA storage builds consistently surprise to the upside in current oversupply regime.

commodities.name: UNG Natural Gas ETF — Long Puts
commodities.rationale: UNG tracks front-month nat gas; direct put on the ETF is simpler than futures for equity account holders while providing equivalent downside exposure to the fundamental thesis.

fixed_income.name: HY Energy Bonds — Bear Spread
fixed_income.entry: Buy HYG $79 put / Sell HYG $74 put
fixed_income.rationale: High yield energy bonds deteriorate as nat gas prices collapse; E&P companies in the high yield index with gas-heavy production see free cash flow compress — HYG put spread hedges credit stress.

fixed_income.name: BIL Short-Term Treasuries — Long
fixed_income.rationale: During commodity stress, rotating into T-bills captures 5%+ risk-free return while avoiding energy credit exposure; defensive parking while the gas breakdown plays out.

currencies.name: Canadian Dollar — Bear Puts
currencies.rationale: Canada is a major natural gas exporter; nat gas price collapse reduces Canadian energy export revenues and trade surplus — CAD depreciates alongside gas in sustained downtrends.

currencies.name: Norwegian Krone — Bear Play
currencies.rationale: Norway's petroleum revenues include significant natural gas; Equinor exports to European markets — NOK weakens when European gas TTF prices fall, which correlates with US Henry Hub directionally.

equities.name: EQT Corporation — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: EQT is the largest US natural gas producer — pure-play exposure to Henry Hub prices; every $0.50/mcf nat gas decline reduces EQT's EBITDA by ~$400M, putting the $45 put in the money quickly.

equities.name: AR Antero Resources — Bear Puts
equities.rationale: Antero is highly levered to nat gas prices with significant Appalachian production; leverage amplifies downside — AR typically moves 2–3x the percentage change in Henry Hub prices.

title: Financials Debt Stress Fade
desc: Rising yields + sovereign and corporate debt creating fragility. Regional banks and leveraged sectors most exposed to a credit event.
strategy: Long puts on financials / bear put spreads

commodities.name: GLD Gold ETF — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: Credit stress events historically trigger gold rallies as institutional investors seek assets with no counterparty risk; GLD calls provide the crisis hedge if financial stress escalates.

commodities.name: VIX — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: Financial sector stress is the most reliable trigger for VIX spikes; bank-specific credit events in 2023 drove VIX from 18 to 26 in 72 hours — cheap protection against tail acceleration.

fixed_income.name: HYG High Yield ETF — Bear Put Spread
fixed_income.rationale: High yield credit spreads are the leading indicator of bank stress; HYG widens before bank stocks fall — put spreads on HYG provide early-warning protection on the debt stress thesis.

fixed_income.name: LQD Investment Grade ETF — Bear Puts
fixed_income.rationale: IG credit spreads also widen in financial stress events; LQD puts capture the systemic contagion from regional banks into broader credit markets via spread widening.

currencies.name: Japanese Yen — Long (Risk-Off)
currencies.rationale: Financial stress events trigger unwinding of JPY carry trades; USD/JPY fell 10% in 2 weeks during the August 2024 carry trade unwind — JPY calls provide asymmetric protection against bank-driven risk-off.

currencies.name: US Dollar — Long (Flight to Quality)
currencies.rationale: Dollar strengthens in US financial stress events as international investors seek the deepest, most liquid safe haven; paradoxically, US bank stress makes the dollar stronger in the short term.

equities.name: XLF Financial Sector ETF — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: XLF is at the intersection of rising rates, CRE exposure, and deposit flight risk — the $44 target captures a 10% correction which is the average drawdown in prior regional bank stress events.

equities.name: KRE Regional Bank ETF — Long Puts
equities.rationale: Regional banks hold 70% of CRE loans; KRE is the most concentrated vehicle for the credit stress thesis — pure exposure to the institutions most vulnerable to the CRE rollover wave.

title: Consumer Spending Slowdown
desc: Sticky inflation eroding real wages, high rates squeezing credit, confidence weakening. Discretionary spending is the marginal victim.
strategy: Bear put spreads on consumer discretionary sector

commodities.name: Coffee Futures — Bear Spread
commodities.rationale: Premium food spending is the first discretionary cut when budgets tighten; coffee shop traffic data (Starbucks comps) declines ahead of broader discretionary spending — coffee futures reflect the margin squeeze.

commodities.name: Agricultural ETF — Bear Position
commodities.rationale: MOO tracks agricultural producers who sell to food retailers; consumer pullback from premium food products compresses volumes and pricing power across the agricultural value chain.

fixed_income.name: HYG High Yield ETF — Bear Put Spread
fixed_income.rationale: Consumer company high yield bonds (retail, restaurant, consumer services) deteriorate as same-store sales decline; HYG spread widening leads equity weakness by 4–6 weeks.

fixed_income.name: JNK High Yield ETF — Long Puts
fixed_income.rationale: JNK has heavier exposure to consumer-facing high yield names than HYG; put directly on the consumer credit stress thesis as delinquency rates rise and refinancing costs spike.

currencies.name: Mexican Peso — Neutral to Bear
currencies.rationale: US consumer weakness reduces cross-border remittances and border manufacturing demand; MXN is highly correlated with US consumer health as Mexico exports primarily to US consumers.

currencies.name: US Dollar — Tactical Long
currencies.rationale: Consumer weakness eventually forces Fed dovishness, which is dollar-negative long-term; but in the near term, risk-off from consumer slowdown creates dollar safe-haven bids.

equities.name: XRT Retail ETF — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: XRT is an equal-weight retail ETF with high exposure to mid-tier and specialty retailers — precisely the segment that loses share first when consumer budgets tighten from credit/inflation pressure.

equities.name: XLY Consumer Discretionary ETF — Bear Puts
equities.rationale: XLY contains Amazon (45% weight), which hedges pure-play risk, but the basket's 30+ discretionary retailers face severe same-store sales pressure from the budget-squeeze thesis.

title: Long Bond Structural Short
desc: Debt monetization, persistent inflation, and structural fiscal deficits keep upward pressure on long yields. TLT range-bound to lower.
strategy: Bear put spreads on TLT, roll monthly on relief bounces

commodities.name: TIPS Inflation-Protected Securities — Long
commodities.entry: Buy TIP as hedge against long-bond inflation risk
commodities.rationale: TIPS are the natural complement to a long-bond short; if inflation stays sticky (which drives yields higher), TIPS outperform nominal bonds — holding TIPS funds the cash collateral for TLT puts.

commodities.name: Gold Futures — Long (Inflation Parallel)
commodities.entry: Long 1 micro gold contract per $5,000 portfolio
commodities.rationale: The same inflationary forces that break the long-bond bull market drive gold; gold and TLT move inversely in inflation regimes — long gold finances a structural TLT short conceptually.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Long-Dated Bear Puts
fixed_income.rationale: Core structural short on the long bond; 6-month expiry survives near-term tactical rallies while capturing the structural trend. Roll quarterly to maintain persistent short duration thesis.

fixed_income.name: TBT 2x Short TLT ETF — Long Calls
fixed_income.rationale: TBT provides 2x daily inverse exposure to TLT; buying calls on TBT provides 2x leveraged bearish bonds with defined risk — powerful structure for the long bond structural short thesis.

currencies.name: US Dollar — Long vs JPY and EUR
currencies.rationale: Higher US yields support dollar versus negative-yielding JPY and sub-4% EUR rates; the yield differential trade has been the most reliable currency carry of the past 3 years.

currencies.name: EM Currencies — Bear Basket
currencies.rationale: EM currencies weaken as US long yields rise; higher Treasury yields drain capital from EM into US bonds — the structural yield re-rating is the most reliable EM bear signal in modern history.

equities.name: XLU Utilities ETF — Long-Dated Bear Puts
equities.rationale: Utilities are the most rate-sensitive equity sector; XLU's 3% dividend yield becomes unattractive versus 4.31% risk-free Treasuries — multiple compression is structural as long as yields stay high.

equities.name: Dividend ETF — Bear Puts
equities.rationale: High-dividend stocks are bond proxies; as Treasury yields rise above dividend yields, institutional capital rotates away from DVY into Treasuries — rate-driven multiple compression is the structural mechanism.

title: Sovereign Debt Crisis Tail Risk
desc: Record global debt + rising yields = compounding servicing costs. A credit event or policy mistake could trigger severe dislocation.
strategy: Long-dated straddles / tail risk puts on financials

commodities.name: Gold Futures — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: Sovereign debt crises historically trigger gold revaluation; the $5,000 target has 30%+ probability if a fiscal policy shock materializes — long-dated calls provide time for the tail to develop.

commodities.name: WTI Crude — Long Straddle
commodities.rationale: A debt/fiscal crisis creates a highly binary oil outcome: recession (oil falls) or monetization/inflation (oil spikes); a straddle profits from either large directional move — the size of the move, not direction, is the bet.

fixed_income.name: TLT Long Bond ETF — Long-Dated Puts
fixed_income.rationale: The sovereign debt crisis scenario implies forced yield spikes; long-dated TLT puts survive tactical rallies and capture the ultimate repricing — these are structural insurance, not momentum trades.

fixed_income.name: HYG High Yield ETF — Long Puts
fixed_income.rationale: Credit spreads widen dramatically in sovereign stress events; HY bonds are first to sell off as risk appetite collapses — long-dated HYG puts are cheap insurance against a systemic credit event.

currencies.name: Swiss Franc — Long vs USD
currencies.rationale: CHF is the deepest safe haven in a dollar stress scenario; the SNB maintains a strong CHF policy as a feature not a bug — in US sovereign stress, CHF appreciates significantly against all fiat currencies.

currencies.name: Gold-Backed FX Basket — Long
currencies.rationale: AUD (gold producer) + CHF (hard currency) forms the strongest currency combination against a US debt debasement scenario; both appreciate for different but complementary reasons in the tail scenario.

equities.name: XLF Financial Sector ETF — Long-Dated Puts
equities.rationale: Financial sector holds the most sovereign debt exposure via Treasury portfolios; in a US fiscal crisis, banks face mark-to-market losses simultaneously with a credit crunch — XLF is the highest-conviction equity short.

equities.name: SPY S&P 500 ETF — Long-Dated Tail Puts
equities.rationale: Tail risk puts on the broad market; 16% OTM puts at 12 months cost 1.5% of notional — cheap insurance for a scenario that is low probability but high magnitude if it occurs.

title: Fossil Fuel Secular Decline
desc: Regulatory pressure, energy transition, and AI-accelerated clean tech create decade-long structural headwinds for fossil fuel infrastructure.
strategy: Bear put spreads on nat gas, calendar spreads to sell rallies

commodities.name: NG=F Natural Gas Futures — Bear Puts
commodities.rationale: Structural fossil fuel decline is most advanced in natural gas as solar/wind outcompete gas generation on a LCOE basis; the 6-month expiry allows the thesis to develop through seasonal demand variations.

commodities.name: USO Oil ETF — Long-Dated Bear Puts
commodities.rationale: Oil demand peak is a 5–10 year horizon thesis, but option market pricing doesn't fully reflect it; long-dated OTM puts are cheap insurance as each annual EV sales record incrementally prices in the terminal demand decline.

fixed_income.name: Fossil Fuel Corporate Bonds — Short (via HYG)
fixed_income.rationale: HY energy bonds embed no transition risk premium; as fossil fuel capex declines, energy companies' cash flow deteriorates — HY bonds will reprice to reflect stranded asset risk over a 3–5 year horizon.

fixed_income.name: Green Bonds ETF — Long
fixed_income.rationale: Long green bonds / short fossil fuel bonds is the fixed income expression of the energy transition; green bond spreads are tightening as ESG capital mandates grow — BGRN benefits from both demand-driven spread compression and the structural shift.

currencies.name: Norwegian Krone — Structural Short
currencies.rationale: Norway's sovereign wealth fund is the world's largest, built entirely on oil revenues; as fossil fuel income declines structurally, NOK loses its fundamental support — a 10-year structural short thesis.

currencies.name: Saudi Riyal — Indirect Hedge
currencies.entry: Tactically short SAR exposure via EM FX
currencies.rationale: Saudi Arabia's currency is pegged but its fiscal breakeven is $80+ oil; structural oil demand decline threatens peg sustainability long-term — indirect expression via Saudi-correlated EM currencies.

equities.name: XOP Oil & Gas E&P ETF — Long-Dated Puts
equities.rationale: E&P companies face a double headwind: structural demand decline means lower long-term oil prices, and ESG capital mandates mean higher cost of capital — both compress equity valuations regardless of near-term commodity prices.

equities.name: KMI Kinder Morgan — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: Pipeline MLPs are stranded asset pure-plays; new gas pipeline permitting is effectively impossible, and existing infrastructure becomes increasingly uneconomic as gas demand peaks and declines over the next decade.

title: Traditional Finance Disintermediation
desc: Rising rates squeezing bank margins and CRE stress creating multi-year headwinds for legacy financials.
strategy: Long puts on financials sector, roll quarterly

commodities.name: Gold ETF — Long (Alternative Store of Value)
commodities.rationale: Gold is the original disintermediation — an asset with no counterparty risk; as trust in traditional financial intermediaries erodes, gold allocation increases structurally across institutional and retail portfolios.

commodities.name: Bitcoin Proxy — Long (via BITO ETF)
commodities.rationale: Crypto is the digital-native disintermediation play; as traditional finance loses its intermediary franchise, Bitcoin narratively and practically gains allocation — BITO provides exposure without self-custody.

fixed_income.name: HYG High Yield ETF — Bear Put Spread
fixed_income.rationale: HY bonds issued by traditional financial sector companies deteriorate as disintermediation accelerates — bank holding company bonds are the most exposed to the revenue model disruption.

fixed_income.name: Bank Bonds — Short Relative to Private Credit
fixed_income.entry: Short bank bonds vs long private credit funds
fixed_income.rationale: Traditional bank bonds trade as if their franchise value is intact; private credit market share gains make this a widening spread trade — bank bond spreads should converge upward to reflect business model risk.

currencies.name: US Dollar — Tactical Long
currencies.rationale: Dollar paradoxically strengthens in US financial stress events short-term; disintermediation-driven bank stress creates risk-off dollar bids even as the long-term trend may be dollar negative from debt monetization.

currencies.name: Swiss Franc — Structural Long
currencies.rationale: Swiss banking system maintained through financial crises via strict capital requirements; CHF as a hard currency benefits from global confidence erosion in fractional reserve banking systems.

equities.name: XLF Financial Sector ETF — Long-Dated Bear Puts
equities.rationale: XLF captures the full traditional finance sector; private credit disruption, CRE losses, and rate squeeze are three simultaneous headwinds — 6-month puts allow the revenue compression to materialize in quarterly earnings.

equities.name: KBWB Banking ETF — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: KBWB is pure banking sector exposure (no insurance or asset management diversification); as loan market share migrates to private credit and deposits are competed away by money market funds, KBWB is the highest-conviction equity short on the disintermediation thesis.

title: India Oversold Relief Bounce
desc: Nifty RSI 37.5, Bank Nifty RSI 35 — deeply oversold. MACD histogram flattening. Mean reversion for a tactical 5–8% bounce only — not a structural view.
strategy: Near-term bull call spread on Nifty, tight stops

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Tactical Hedge
commodities.entry: Buy MCX Gold as portfolio hedge
commodities.rationale: During India equity bounces, some capital rotates from gold back to equities — but holding MCX Gold as a hedge limits downside if the 'bounce' fails and the selloff resumes; partial hedge, not conviction short.

commodities.name: Agri Commodities — Neutral/Long
commodities.rationale: Agricultural commodity prices in India often stabilize during equity recoveries; rural income improvement from stable agri prices supports the consumer-driven bounce thesis.

fixed_income.name: India 10Y Government Bond — Long
fixed_income.rationale: Bond yields stabilize as equity panic subsides; at 7%+ yield, India Gsecs offer attractive risk/reward as the relief bounce reduces risk premium and bonds rally alongside equities.

fixed_income.name: INDA India ETF Bond Component — Long
fixed_income.rationale: INDA (US-listed India ETF) captures the equity bounce with US-listed liquidity; partial bond exposure within the fund provides ballast if the equity bounce is smaller than anticipated.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Bear Spread (INR Recovery)
currencies.rationale: India equity relief bounces historically correlate with INR recovery as FII outflows pause; USD/INR typically drops 1–2% in the first week of a significant Nifty recovery.

currencies.name: EM Currency Basket — Long
currencies.entry: Buy EEM as India bounce proxy
currencies.rationale: India equity bounces often coincide with broader EM relief rallies; EEM provides correlated exposure to the EM risk-on thesis without pure India single-country risk.

equities.name: INDA India ETF — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: Defined-risk expression of the India bounce thesis; $51 captures a 9% Nifty bounce from oversold levels — the target is realistic given RSI 37.5 starting point and historical mean-reversion magnitude.

equities.name: Nifty Bank Bull Call Spread — India-Listed
equities.entry: Buy ATM call / Sell 7% OTM call
equities.rationale: Bank Nifty typically leads Nifty recoveries; RSI 35 is the deepest oversold level since March 2023 — a 7% bounce is the base case that the spread captures with defined risk.

title: Gold MCX Spike — INR Amplification
desc: Gold +75% in USD. In INR terms, amplified by INR depreciation (+10% 1Y). India as world's largest gold consumer sees domestic demand surge on geo events.
strategy: Long calls on MCX Gold / Gold ETFs listed in India

commodities.name: MCX Gold Futures — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: INR-denominated gold call options provide leveraged exposure to both USD gold price appreciation AND further INR depreciation — double benefit from the same underlying position.

commodities.name: Gold ETF India — Long
commodities.rationale: Gold ETFs in India are the simplest way to hold gold without storage costs; NAV tracks MCX Gold directly, providing the INR-amplified return without futures margin calls or delivery complexity.

fixed_income.name: Sovereign Gold Bond — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy existing SGBs in secondary market
fixed_income.rationale: SGBs pay 2.5% annual interest IN ADDITION to gold price appreciation; they are government-guaranteed gold exposure with a yield kicker — the best risk/reward gold instrument available in India.

fixed_income.name: TIPS ETF (US) — Long (Inflation Parallel)
fixed_income.entry: Buy TIP as US-listed inflation hedge
fixed_income.rationale: US TIPS benefit from the same inflation dynamic driving gold; holding both provides portfolio-level inflation hedging across both USD and INR currency dimensions.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long Calls (Depreciation Play)
currencies.rationale: The same forces driving gold (geopolitics, oil spike, inflation) are driving INR depreciation; USD/INR calls amplify the gold thesis by capturing the currency component separately.

currencies.name: AUD/INR — Long (Commodity Currency)
currencies.rationale: AUD benefits from gold production; INR weakens from oil imports; the AUD/INR cross captures both sides of the gold thesis — commodity currency strengthening vs import-dependent currency weakening.

equities.name: GDX Gold Miners ETF (US-listed) — Long Calls
equities.rationale: GDX miners have operating leverage to gold prices; Indian investors can access GDX through US-listed accounts — miners amplify the gold thesis by 2–3x on price moves.

equities.name: INDA — Long (INR Depreciation + Gold Theme)
equities.rationale: INDA contains gold mining and materials companies; US-listed, so gains from INR depreciation in USD-denominated NAV are retained — partial gold thesis expression within broader India allocation.

title: India VIX Premium Harvest
desc: India VIX up 117% in 1 month — historically extreme. When India VIX peaks and mean-reverts, option premium selling becomes highly attractive.
strategy: Iron condors / short strangles on Nifty after VIX peaks

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Iron Condor
commodities.entry: Sell OTM call + OTM put on MCX Gold
commodities.rationale: MCX Gold vol is also elevated in the fear environment; selling both sides of gold captures premium decay while gold consolidates — manage at 50% profit or close if gold moves more than 5%.

commodities.name: MCX Silver — Short Strangle
commodities.rationale: Silver's elevated IV creates attractive strangle premium; sell at 30-delta strikes that give 70%+ probability of profit — size small given silver's higher volatility versus gold.

fixed_income.name: India Short-Term Government Bills — Long
fixed_income.rationale: T-bill proceeds fund the margin for options strategies; earning 7% on the cash collateral while selling Nifty vol is the complete strategy — total return = option premium + T-bill yield.

fixed_income.name: Liquid Funds — Overnight Sweep
fixed_income.entry: Park non-deployed capital in liquid funds
fixed_income.rationale: Indian liquid funds yield 6.8–7% with daily liquidity; they serve as the cash management vehicle for the options trading capital, maximizing total return in the high-vol premium harvesting strategy.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Iron Condor
currencies.entry: Sell OTM call + OTM put on USD/INR options
currencies.rationale: USD/INR vol is also elevated; INR is stuck in a range as RBI actively defends the 93–94 level — selling vol in this range-bound environment is the highest-probability currency strategy available.

currencies.name: EUR/INR — Short Strangle
currencies.rationale: EUR/INR has lower realized vol than USD/INR but similar implied vol — better risk/reward for premium selling; ECB rate trajectory and RBI intervention create natural range bounds.

equities.name: Nifty 50 — Iron Condor
equities.rationale: The highest-probability India VIX premium harvest; at VIX 25.52, Nifty weekly strangles collect 40%+ more premium than at VIX 15 — iron condor defines the maximum loss while capturing the elevated premium.

equities.name: Bank Nifty — Short Strangle
equities.rationale: Bank Nifty has 25–30% higher vol than Nifty; weekly options generate more premium per unit of time — manage at 50% profit or roll if either side is tested. Do not carry into RBI policy announcements.

title: PSU Bank Tactical Bounce
desc: PSU banks disproportionately punished. With RBI rate cut cycle potentially beginning and credit quality intact, a tactical relief rally is plausible.
strategy: Bull call spreads on Bank Nifty with defined risk

commodities.name: MCX Copper — Neutral/Tactical Long
commodities.rationale: PSU bank bounce thesis requires India economic recovery; copper is the best real-time indicator of India's industrial and construction credit demand — rising copper validates the PSU bank recovery thesis.

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Neutral (Hedge Position)
commodities.entry: Reduce gold allocation as banks recover
commodities.rationale: During PSU bank bounces, capital rotates FROM gold to equities; reducing MCX gold allocation funds the bank equity trade while managing total portfolio risk during the rotation.

fixed_income.name: SBI Bonds — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy SBI perpetual or tier-2 bonds
fixed_income.rationale: SBI bonds provide fixed income exposure to the PSU bank recovery thesis; bond prices rally as credit quality improves and spreads compress — captures the same thesis with lower volatility than equity.

fixed_income.name: India 10Y Gsec — Long
fixed_income.rationale: RBI rate cuts would drive Gsec price appreciation simultaneously with PSU bank equity gains; long Gsecs hedge the scenario where the RBI cuts before the equity bounce fully materializes.

currencies.name: INR — Long vs USD (Recovery Play)
currencies.rationale: PSU bank recoveries coincide with FII inflow reversal which strengthens INR; the INR put is a natural complement to the bank equity long — if banks recover, FII flows return, and INR appreciates.

currencies.name: INR vs EM Basket — Long
currencies.entry: Long INR vs underperforming EM currencies
currencies.rationale: India's relative macro stability versus Brazil, Turkey, or South Africa makes INR the highest-quality EM long in a risk-on environment — PSU bank recovery supports this relative value positioning.

equities.name: BANKBEES — Bull Call Spread
equities.entry: Buy ATM call / Sell 7% OTM call
equities.rationale: BANKBEES is the Bank Nifty ETF; buying near-dated bull call spreads captures the tactical bounce while selling the OTM call manages cost — 7% target aligns with the historical mean-reversion magnitude from RSI 35.

equities.name: SBI — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: SBI is the highest-quality PSU bank at <0.9x book; any RBI rate cut or FII flow reversal catalyst triggers disproportionate SBI outperformance — the $850 target represents book value, a natural resistance and target.

title: India Infrastructure Supercycle
desc: Government capex targeting $1.4T over 5 years in roads, rail, ports, and smart cities. Multi-year demand for capital goods, cement, and construction.
strategy: Bull call spreads on infrastructure / capital goods sector

commodities.name: MCX Copper — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: India's infrastructure supercycle is the single largest domestic copper demand driver; 60,000km of new highways, 500+ metro stations, and port electrification create multi-year demand growth exceeding current MCX pricing.

commodities.name: Iron & Steel ETF — Long (Infrastructure Materials)
commodities.entry: Buy SAIL or Tata Steel on weakness
commodities.rationale: Steel is the primary construction input for infrastructure; India's per-capita steel consumption at 85kg vs global average 230kg confirms there is a decade of catch-up consumption ahead — infrastructure supercycle drives the gap.

fixed_income.name: India Infrastructure Bonds — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy IRFC or NHAI bonds in secondary market
fixed_income.rationale: IRFC (Indian Railway Finance Corp) and NHAI bonds are government-guaranteed infrastructure debt; yields of 7.5–8% with sovereign-equivalent credit quality make these the best risk/reward in Indian fixed income.

fixed_income.name: Tax-Free Infrastructure Bonds — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy NHAI/REC tax-free bonds in secondary market
fixed_income.rationale: Tax-free infrastructure bonds offer 8.5%+ gross equivalent yield for 30% tax bracket investors; government-guaranteed, long-duration, and capital gain eligible — the ideal fixed income holding for the multi-year infrastructure thesis.

currencies.name: INR — Long Structural
currencies.rationale: Infrastructure capex attracts FDI inflows from Japan, UAE, and European infrastructure funds; sustained capital inflows provide structural INR support — the long-dated put captures this appreciation over a multi-year horizon.

currencies.name: EUR/INR — Bear (EUR Weakness vs INR Strength)
currencies.rationale: European capital flows into Indian infrastructure (German, French infra funds) create EUR selling / INR buying pressure; the EUR/INR cross captures the currency impact of European infrastructure FDI.

equities.name: INDA India ETF — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: INDA provides US-listed exposure to India's infrastructure beneficiaries (L&T, BHEL, Power Grid, etc.); 12-month bull spread captures the multi-quarter earnings rerating as infrastructure contracts are awarded and executed.

equities.name: L&T — Long Calls (India-Listed)
equities.rationale: L&T is the highest-quality India infrastructure conglomerate with 50%+ of revenues from government infrastructure contracts; order book visibility extends 3+ years, providing earnings predictability that justifies premium valuation.

title: India Renewable Energy Build
desc: India targeting 500GW renewable by 2030. Policy support + global green capital flows. Solar, wind, and storage are decade-long structural themes.
strategy: Bull call spreads on renewable energy sector names

commodities.name: MCX Silver — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: India's solar panel deployment creates years of silver demand growth; MCX Silver provides INR-denominated exposure to global silver prices with the additional INR depreciation amplification that benefits Indian holders.

commodities.name: MCX Copper — Long (Grid Infrastructure)
commodities.rationale: Renewable grid interconnection cables are 60–70% copper by weight; India's transmission line buildout for renewable integration creates the most reliable domestic copper demand growth signal in the commodity complex.

fixed_income.name: IREDA Green Bonds — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy IREDA bonds in secondary market
fixed_income.rationale: IREDA (Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency) finances India's solar and wind projects; bonds are government-supported with growing ESG investor demand — yields compress as global green bond capital pursues India's scale.

fixed_income.name: ICLN Clean Energy ETF (US-listed) — Long
fixed_income.rationale: ICLN provides US-listed exposure to global clean energy companies benefiting from India's 500GW buildout; US-listed for tax efficiency, with India-related revenue exposure through Indian renewable subsidiaries of global companies.

currencies.name: INR — Long (Green Capital Inflows)
currencies.rationale: European pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and development banks are committing $100B+ to India's renewable sector; this sustained FDI creates long-term INR buying pressure — structural currency support from the energy transition thesis.

currencies.name: EUR/INR — Bear (European Solar FDI)
currencies.entry: Sell EUR forward / Buy INR forward
currencies.rationale: European solar and wind companies (Vestas, Siemens Gamesa) winning India tenders convert EUR to INR; these large project payments create recurring EUR/INR selling pressure at predictable intervals.

equities.name: ICLN Clean Energy ETF — Long Calls
equities.rationale: ICLN contains global renewable energy companies with significant India exposure; US-listed liquidity with 6-month calls allows time for India's quarterly capacity addition data to drive earnings upgrades.

equities.name: Adani Green Energy — Long (India-Listed)
equities.rationale: Adani Green is India's largest renewable energy developer with 20GW operational + 50GW in pipeline; at current capacity addition rates, Adani Green will be among the world's 5 largest renewable companies by 2030 — early-stage compounding opportunity.

title: India Financial Credit Growth
desc: India's credit penetration remains low vs GDP. Structural growth in retail loans, SME credit, and insurance over decades. Multi-decade compounders.
strategy: Covered calls on financial sector long positions

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Strategic Allocation
commodities.rationale: Indian household savings are 60%+ in gold; as financial credit grows and Indians formalize savings, initial gold preferences transition to diversified assets — gold is the starting point of India's financial deepening journey.

commodities.name: Agri Commodity Index — Long
commodities.rationale: Rural credit growth (Kisan credit cards, agri loans) is the fastest-growing segment; agri commodity price stability validates the rural income improvement thesis that drives rural banking expansion.

fixed_income.name: Indian Corporate Bonds — Long
fixed_income.rationale: Credit market deepening is the thesis — India's corporate bond market at 17% of GDP will grow to 30%+ as institutional depth increases; early positioning captures both yield and spread compression as the market matures.

fixed_income.name: HDFC NCD — Long
fixed_income.entry: Buy HDFC or Bajaj Finance NCD
fixed_income.rationale: HDFC's NCDs are AAA-rated with 8.5% yield; as India's largest housing finance company, HDFC is the direct beneficiary of the mortgage penetration growth thesis — bond exposure provides yield while equity exposure provides capital appreciation.

currencies.name: INR — Structural Long
currencies.rationale: Financial sector deepening attracts multi-year FDI from global private equity, strategic investors, and development banks; this sustained capital inflow creates a structural INR appreciation floor over a 3–5 year period.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Income Carry
currencies.rationale: INR yields 6.5–7% vs USD yields 5.25%; the positive carry of being long INR is attractive in periods of relative macrostability — the credit growth thesis reduces INR volatility, making the carry more reliable.

equities.name: HDFC Bank — Bull Call Spread
equities.rationale: HDFC Bank is India's highest-quality private bank; at 2.2x book with 20%+ ROE and 18% credit CAGR, the valuation implies almost no premium for the decade-long growth runway — the 12-month bull spread captures the re-rating as the selloff reverses.

equities.name: BANKBEES — Calendar Spread
equities.rationale: BANKBEES calendar spread is the most capital-efficient way to hold a 6-month India financial sector view while generating monthly premium income; the monthly short call decays while the 6-month call captures the structural re-rating.

title: Silver MCX — Solar & Industrial
desc: India's massive solar panel deployment creates decades of silver demand. Domestically priced in INR with currency amplification. Structural long.
strategy: Long calls on MCX Silver, roll on strength

commodities.name: MCX Silver Futures — Long Calls
commodities.rationale: MCX Silver is the most direct expression of the solar+INR amplification thesis; the quarterly contract avoids monthly rollover friction while capturing the 3-month solar demand news flow cycle.

commodities.name: PSLV Physical Silver Trust (US-listed) — Long
commodities.rationale: PSLV holds physical allocated silver with no counterparty risk; during physical tightness episodes, PSLV's premium to NAV expands — providing an additional return layer on top of spot silver appreciation.

fixed_income.name: IREDA Solar Bonds — Long (Silver Demand Proxy)
fixed_income.rationale: IREDA finances the solar projects that drive silver demand; holding IREDA bonds is a fixed income expression of the solar boom thesis — the more capacity that gets financed, the more silver gets consumed.

fixed_income.name: Sovereign Gold Bond — Long (Precious Metals Relative)
fixed_income.entry: Buy SGBs as gold hedge vs silver position
fixed_income.rationale: Gold/silver ratio at 64:1 historically reverts to 45–50:1 in industrial demand cycles; holding silver vs gold SGBs captures the silver outperformance while providing portfolio ballast through gold's monetary safe-haven role.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long Calls (Silver Amplification)
currencies.rationale: Structural INR weakness is the amplifier for MCX Silver returns; holding USD/INR calls alongside MCX Silver doubles the currency component of the silver return — a natural hedge that also generates carry.

currencies.name: AUD/INR — Long (Mining Nation Currency)
currencies.rationale: Australia produces 15% of global silver; AUD appreciates when silver and precious metals are in bull cycles while INR weakens from oil imports — this cross captures both sides of the silver supply/demand dynamic.

equities.name: SLV Silver ETF (US-listed) — Long
equities.rationale: SLV holds physical silver with daily liquidity; US-listed for account compatibility — provides exposure to USD silver prices with the option to hedge or not hedge the USD/INR component based on individual currency view.

equities.name: Hindustan Zinc — Long (India-Listed)
equities.rationale: Hindustan Zinc produces both zinc and silver in India — 500+ tonnes of silver annually as a byproduct; as silver prices rise, HZL's realized prices improve, driving earnings upgrades for a company that screens as deeply undervalued at current silver prices.

title: India IT AI Services Pivot (Selective)
desc: While IT broadly faces disruption, companies pivoting to AI consulting and implementation will capture the transition. Selective long within broadly short sector.
strategy: Bull call spreads on AI-pivot IT leaders vs sector shorts

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Tactical Hedge
commodities.entry: Small gold allocation as tail hedge
commodities.rationale: IT sector recovery can reverse quickly if AI disruption narrative accelerates; gold hedge provides portfolio protection if the selective bull thesis fails and IT continues lower.

commodities.name: Data Center Power — Indirect Commodity
commodities.entry: Small nat gas position as AI infrastructure proxy
commodities.rationale: AI data centers that Indian IT companies serve require power infrastructure; nat gas futures serve as a partial hedge — if AI infrastructure buildout slows, both data center spend and Indian IT demand soften simultaneously.

fixed_income.name: US IG Tech Bonds — Long (Dollar Income)
fixed_income.type: Fixed Income ETF · IG Corporate Bonds
fixed_income.entry: Buy US IG tech corporate bonds
fixed_income.rationale: Indian IT companies' largest clients are US tech giants (Microsoft, Amazon, Google) whose bonds yield 5%+; holding client company bonds as a fixed income expression of the AI services demand thesis provides yield while the IT equity thesis develops.

fixed_income.name: Infosys/TCS Bonds — Long (India Corporate)
fixed_income.entry: Buy Infosys or TCS AA+ bonds
fixed_income.rationale: TCS and Infosys issue AA+ rated bonds in India; their credit quality is improving as AI revenue diversifies and grows — bond prices should appreciate as the equity re-rating also improves the credit narrative.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Short (AI Revenue Tailwind for INR)
currencies.rationale: Indian IT companies earn primarily in USD; as AI-related revenue grows, the USD inflows to India increase — this is a structural INR positive that competes with oil import pressures to determine the net USD/INR direction.

currencies.name: USD — Long for Dollar Revenue Capture
currencies.rationale: Indian IT companies bill in USD; if AI revenue grows, the dollar earnings benefit is real — holding USD provides a natural hedge against the INR component of the trade while the USD billing rate compounds.

equities.name: Infosys — Bull Call Spread (NSE)
equities.rationale: Infosys is the highest-quality AI pivot company among Indian IT majors — $2B+ in AI contract wins in FY24; at 18x earnings, the valuation offers a significant discount to US AI services peers at 25–30x on similar growth rates.

equities.name: Nifty IT ETF — Long Calls (Selective Index)
equities.ticker: NIFTYBEES IT / Nifty IT Index Options
equities.entry: Buy ATM call on Nifty IT Index (6-month)
equities.rationale: Nifty IT options provide sector exposure without single-stock risk; the 6-month call allows time for the AI pivot narrative to drive earnings upgrades across the selective leaders that dominate the index weighting.

title: India Broad Market Selloff Continues
desc: Nifty and Sensex in strong downtrends, below all MAs, MACD deeply negative. FII outflows accelerating. India VIX surging. Near-term pain has further to run.
strategy: Bear put spreads on Nifty / Bank Nifty, near-dated expiry

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Long Calls (Safe Haven)
commodities.rationale: Indian equity selloffs drive domestic safe-haven flows into MCX Gold; simultaneously, global risk-off (which is driving FII outflows) also bids USD gold — double demand driver makes this the highest-conviction commodity hedge.

commodities.name: MCX Crude Oil — Long (Import Bill Hedge)
commodities.rationale: Rising oil prices are a primary driver of India's selloff (via CAD widening and INR weakness); being long MCX crude hedges the portfolio against the dominant macro headwind rather than fighting it.

fixed_income.name: India 10Y Gsec — Short (Rising Yields)
fixed_income.entry: Short 10Y Gsec (or buy inverse bond fund)
fixed_income.rationale: Equity selloffs in India driven by FII outflows often pressure Indian bonds simultaneously; rising USD/INR reduces RBI's capacity to cut rates, keeping yields elevated — bond shorts complement equity shorts.

fixed_income.name: INDA — Bear Put Spread (US-listed)
fixed_income.rationale: INDA is US-listed, capturing both Nifty decline AND INR depreciation simultaneously; as an Indian equity bear, INDA puts are the most efficient hedge — every 1% Nifty decline + 0.5% INR decline = 1.5% INDA decline.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long Calls
currencies.rationale: FII outflows directly drive USD/INR higher; USD/INR call is the most direct currency expression of the India selloff thesis — each ₹1 depreciation represents a 1.1% USD/INR move.

currencies.name: USD — Long vs INR/EM (Safety)
currencies.rationale: In India-specific or broader EM selloffs, the USD strengthens as capital flows to safety; UUP provides broad dollar long exposure that captures the risk-off dynamic driving India's FII outflows.

equities.name: INDA India ETF — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: The most capital-efficient India short — captures both index decline and currency depreciation with defined risk; $40 target implies a further 14% decline which is consistent with historical India bear markets from similar technical starting points.

equities.name: Nifty Bear Put Spread — India-Listed
equities.entry: Buy ATM put / Sell 8% OTM put
equities.rationale: India-listed Nifty puts provide the most direct exposure to the selloff continuation; the 8% OTM short put defines maximum profit at 20,857 — a 8% further decline from current levels that aligns with MACD and MA technical targets.

title: INR Depreciation Accelerating
desc: USD/INR up 10% in 1Y and accelerating. Iran-driven oil spike widening India's import bill. No near-term reversal catalyst visible.
strategy: Long puts on INR-exposed instruments / INDA bear put spread

commodities.name: MCX Crude Oil — Long
commodities.rationale: India's oil import bill drives INR weakness; being long MCX Crude captures both the USD oil price appreciation AND the INR depreciation amplification — a double-leverage play on the primary structural driver of INR weakness.

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Long (INR Depreciation Hedge)
commodities.entry: Buy MCX Gold as depreciation hedge
commodities.rationale: Gold's INR return = USD gold return + INR depreciation; holding MCX Gold provides an automatic currency hedge against INR weakness while also capturing geopolitical safe-haven flows driving USD gold higher.

fixed_income.name: INDA — Bear Position (USD Erosion of INR Returns)
fixed_income.rationale: INDA is USD-priced and holds INR assets; INR depreciation directly reduces INDA's USD NAV even if Nifty is flat in INR terms — INDA puts are a pure currency bet that doesn't require equity market views.

fixed_income.name: India Short-Maturity Gsec — Long (Rate Defense)
fixed_income.rationale: Short-maturity government bills provide high INR yield while limiting duration risk; the RBI will maintain high short rates to defend INR — short-term bills benefit from this policy while avoiding long-end yield spike risk.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long Call Spread
currencies.rationale: The $93–96 range captures the next structural depreciation leg; RBI defends at 93–94, so the risk is limited, but if they allow the INR to slide, the $96 level represents a 3.5% move — asymmetric risk/reward.

currencies.name: EUR/INR — Long (European Currency vs Weakening INR)
currencies.rationale: EUR/INR captures INR weakness without the USD carry cost; European investors hedging India exposure sell EUR/INR — being long the same pair captures the institutional hedging flow as well as the fundamental depreciation.

equities.name: INDA India ETF — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: INDA is the most efficient INR depreciation vehicle in equity markets; every 1% INR depreciation reduces INDA USD NAV by ~1% regardless of Nifty performance — a pure currency depreciation play with defined risk.

equities.name: EEM Emerging Market ETF — Bear Puts
equities.rationale: INR depreciation is often symptomatic of broader EM stress; EEM puts provide a broader EM-level hedge that captures contagion if India's currency weakness spreads to other EM currencies through risk-off sentiment.

title: India IT Near-Term Weakness
desc: Nifty IT down 27% in 1Y and 22% from 52W high. AI disruption narrative + MACD negative + below all MAs. No technical support near-term.
strategy: Bear put spreads on India IT sector / INDA puts

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Long (Safety Allocation)
commodities.rationale: Capital rotating out of India IT seeks safety in gold; holding MCX Gold as the IT short hedge ensures the portfolio benefits from IT weakness regardless of whether the mechanism is FII outflows (gold up) or domestic retail panic (gold up).

commodities.name: Power/Data Center Commodities — Neutral
commodities.rationale: Data center power demand supports some commodity longs, but India IT's revenue comes from servicing — not building — data centers; their weakness doesn't benefit commodity markets in the same way US tech infrastructure would.

fixed_income.name: US IG Tech Bonds — Long (Client vs Vendor)
fixed_income.type: Fixed Income ETF · IG Corporate Bonds
fixed_income.entry: Buy US IG tech bond ETF
fixed_income.rationale: US tech companies (Microsoft, Google) that are automating away Indian IT services are financially stronger as a result; their IG bonds yield 5%+ with improving credit — long the AI beneficiaries' bonds while short the IT vendors' equity.

fixed_income.name: India Gsec Short-Term — Park Capital
fixed_income.entry: Buy T-bills with proceeds from IT position reductions
fixed_income.rationale: Rotating out of IT equities into 7% T-bills generates risk-free returns while waiting for the IT sector bottom; high carry compensates for the opportunity cost of not being invested in IT.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long (IT Sector FII Outflow)
currencies.rationale: IT sector FII outflows are a significant INR negative — as Indian IT loses international investor confidence, FII selling of IT stocks directly weakens INR; USD/INR calls capture the currency side of the IT bear thesis.

currencies.name: USD — Long (Dollar Revenue at Risk)
currencies.rationale: Indian IT earns in USD; if revenue growth decelerates, the USD inflow to India's tech sector slows, reducing the INR support that IT exports historically provided — long USD as a hedge on IT revenue weakness.

equities.name: Infosys — Bear Put Spread (NSE)
equities.rationale: Infosys is the sector bellwether; when Infosys misses revenue guidance (which it has 3 consecutive quarters), the entire sector reprices lower — puts on INFY capture both single-stock and sector-level downside risk.

equities.name: INDA — Bear Put Spread (IT Sector Exposure)
equities.rationale: INDA has 15%+ IT sector weighting; Nifty IT further underperformance of 10% would directly compress INDA NAV by 1.5% from IT alone, plus additional FII outflow impact on INR — efficient and liquid hedge available in US markets.

title: Indian Aviation Oil Squeeze
desc: Oil up 79% in 1 month. Indian aviation exposed to USD-priced jet fuel while INR simultaneously weakens. Margin compression is swift and severe.
strategy: Bear put spreads on aviation / transport sector

commodities.name: MCX Crude Oil — Long (Core Thesis Driver)
commodities.entry: Long MCX Crude as primary position
commodities.rationale: The oil spike is simultaneously the thesis driver (aviation cost squeeze) and the best hedge; being long crude oil while short aviation equities creates a natural pairs trade where oil appreciation funds the short side via commodity gains.

commodities.name: WTI Crude Futures — Long Call
commodities.rationale: Maintaining a crude long confirms the thesis direction — if oil continues higher, aviation losses deepen; if oil reverses, the commodity position limits downside on the aviation short while the thesis potentially exits.

fixed_income.name: Aviation Sector Bonds — Short (via HY proxy)
fixed_income.rationale: Indian aviation companies have high-yield equivalent credit profiles; oil cost spikes compress cash flow, increasing default risk — HY credit spread widening is the fixed income expression of the aviation squeeze thesis.

fixed_income.name: India Gsec — Long (Flight to Safety)
fixed_income.rationale: If aviation sector stress becomes systemic (airline failures, route cancellations), government bonds rally as the safe haven; Gsecs provide ballast to the high-conviction aviation short position.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long (Fuel Cost Amplifier)
currencies.rationale: The aviation squeeze has two amplifiers: oil price (captured by commodity long) and INR depreciation (captured by USD/INR calls); structuring both sides of the amplification thesis maximizes the thesis expression.

currencies.name: USD — Long (Systemic Risk-Off Hedge)
currencies.entry: Hold UUP as tail risk hedge
currencies.rationale: If oil spike + INR weakness creates a systemic India crisis beyond just aviation, USD strengthens as global risk-off accelerates — UUP provides portfolio-level protection against the tail scenario of the thesis.

equities.name: IndiGo (InterGlobe Aviation) — Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: IndiGo has the largest India domestic market share (60%+) but also the highest fuel cost sensitivity; every $10/bbl oil increase reduces IndiGo's quarterly EBIT by ₹800–1,000 crore — at current oil levels, Q2 results should be severely impacted.

equities.name: INDA — Bear Put Spread (Aviation Sector Exposure)
equities.rationale: INDA includes aviation and transport sector companies; oil shock creates broad India negative sentiment beyond just airlines — INDA puts capture the macro impact of the aviation cost squeeze on the broader Indian equity market.

title: India IT Structural Disruption
desc: GenAI fundamentally disrupting Indian IT outsourcing. Clients automating processes previously staffed by Infosys and TCS. Decade-long structural compression.
strategy: Long-dated bear put spreads, roll quarterly as thesis plays out

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Long-Term Strategic Allocation
commodities.entry: Strategic 15% portfolio allocation to MCX Gold
commodities.rationale: As Indian IT loses its structural growth story, capital rotating out of IT should flow into alternative stores of value; gold maintains portfolio returns while IT's decade-long underperformance plays out.

commodities.name: Data Center Power Commodities — Neutral Hedge
commodities.entry: Small copper long as partial hedge
commodities.rationale: AI infrastructure that displaces Indian IT labor consumes copper and power; long copper hedges the scenario where AI buildout accelerates (which accelerates IT disruption) — the commodity position gains from the same force causing IT's decline.

fixed_income.name: US IG Tech Bonds — Long (AI Beneficiary Credit)
fixed_income.type: Fixed Income ETF · IG Corporate Bonds
fixed_income.entry: Buy US IG tech bond ETF
fixed_income.rationale: Microsoft, Google, and Meta bonds yield 5%+ while their AI revenues grow — the structural disruption that harms Indian IT improves these companies' credit profiles; buying AI winners' bonds while shorting IT vendors' equity is the complete trade.

fixed_income.name: India IT Sector Bonds — Neutral (Avoid)
fixed_income.entry: Reduce or exit Indian IT corporate bond exposure
fixed_income.rationale: Indian IT bonds offer 7.5% but the credit narrative is deteriorating as revenue growth decelerates and AI disruption accelerates workforce reductions — credit spreads should widen as the structural story becomes unavoidable to fixed income markets.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long-Dated Calls (IT Revenue Slowdown)
currencies.rationale: Indian IT's dollar earnings slowdown reduces the USD inflow that has historically supported INR; long-dated USD/INR calls capture the structural INR weakening as IT sector USD generation declines over multiple years.

currencies.name: USD — Long (Structural Revenue Transfer)
currencies.rationale: AI tools (primarily USD-denominated) replace Indian IT labor (INR-denominated); this is a structural transfer of IT value-add from INR to USD — long USD is the currency expression of the IT disruption structural thesis.

equities.name: INDA — Long-Dated Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: INDA's 15%+ IT weighting means the structural IT disruption is directly captured; 12-month expiry allows time for 3–4 quarterly earnings misses that confirm the structural revenue deceleration — the definitive equity expression of the 3–5 year IT re-rating thesis.

equities.name: Nifty IT Index — Long-Dated Bear Put Spread
equities.entry: Buy ATM put / Sell 15% OTM put (12-month)
equities.rationale: Long-dated Nifty IT puts survive the quarterly bounces while capturing the structural multi-year re-rating; the 15% OTM short put defines maximum profit at 25,874 — a 15% decline aligned with Accenture-type AI restructuring announcements from Indian IT majors.

title: INR Structural Weakness
desc: India imports 85% of oil, has persistent CAD, and faces FII outflows. INR depreciates every year. Oil crisis accelerates this structural trend.
strategy: Long-dated puts on INDA, calendar spreads on INR pairs

commodities.name: MCX Crude Oil — Long (CAD Driver)
commodities.entry: Strategic long on MCX Crude as CAD proxy
commodities.rationale: India's INR structural weakness is primarily driven by oil import costs; long MCX crude captures the commodity side of the thesis while simultaneously generating returns that partially compensate for INR depreciation on other positions.

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Long-Term Strategic
commodities.rationale: Gold in INR terms appreciates by the USD gold return + INR depreciation rate; a 3–5% annual INR depreciation adds 3–5 percentage points to the MCX Gold return annually — it is the most natural INR depreciation hedge for Indian investors.

fixed_income.name: India 10Y Gsec — Short (Rate Risk)
fixed_income.entry: Short 10Y Gsec or buy inverse bond product
fixed_income.rationale: INR structural weakness limits RBI's ability to cut rates (cutting rates while currency is under pressure would accelerate depreciation); Gsec yields should remain elevated or rise — short position benefits from the rate-currency policy constraint.

fixed_income.name: INDA — Long-Dated Bear Puts (Currency + Equity Combo)
fixed_income.rationale: INDA captures both the Nifty equity decline AND the INR depreciation in one USD-priced instrument; as the structural thesis plays out, every 1% INR depreciation reduces INDA USD NAV by 1% regardless of Nifty performance.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long-Dated Calendar Spread
currencies.entry: Buy 12-month call / Sell 3-month call (to reduce cost)
