currencies.rationale: Calendar spread on USD/INR captures structural depreciation while reducing premium cost by selling nearer-term vol; the structural depreciation trend allows rolling the short leg monthly while maintaining the long-term directional view.

currencies.name: USD vs INR/EM Basket — Long Relative Value
currencies.entry: Long USD / Short EM FX basket including INR
currencies.rationale: INR is one of the highest-yielding carry currencies globally; but when carry trade unwinding occurs, high-yielders (INR, BRL, ZAR) sell off the most — long USD vs EM basket captures both INR structural weakness and systematic carry risk.

equities.name: INDA — Long-Dated Bear Put Spread
equities.rationale: The long-dated INDA put spread is the definitive structural INR weakness trade: it captures equity decline, currency depreciation, and the compounding of both over a 12-month horizon — the time to maturity allows the structural thesis to fully develop.

equities.name: EEM EM ETF — Bear Puts (Contagion Hedge)
equities.rationale: INR structural weakness often signals broader EM stress; EEM puts capture the contagion scenario where India's currency dynamics spread to other high-yield EM currencies, creating a broader EM risk-off event that amplifies the India thesis.

title: Coal & Fossil Displacement
desc: India's 500GW renewable mandate + falling solar costs + global ESG pressure structurally displace coal over the next decade.
strategy: Bear put spreads on coal/legacy energy, calendar spreads on bounces

commodities.name: Coal Futures — Short (Structural Decline)
commodities.rationale: India's renewable additions are creating a structural coal demand ceiling; every GW of solar commissioned reduces coal burn by 1.5–2 million tonnes annually — the demand destruction is compounding and irreversible at the policy level.

commodities.name: Natural Gas — Short (Transition Fuel Ceiling)
commodities.rationale: Gas is supposed to be the 'bridge fuel' in the energy transition, but India's aggressive renewable additions are skipping the gas bridge entirely; demand growth for gas is lower than forecast, and current oversupply will persist structurally.

fixed_income.name: Coal Sector Bonds — Avoid/Short
fixed_income.entry: Exit or avoid all fossil fuel sector bonds
fixed_income.rationale: Coal bonds price in no transition risk despite the structural demand ceiling; as ESG capital mandates grow, fossil fuel bonds will face forced selling and spread widening — the risk/reward of holding high yield without transition risk premium is unfavorable.

fixed_income.name: Green Bonds — Long (Counter-Position)
fixed_income.entry: Buy BGRN or IREDA green bonds
fixed_income.rationale: Green bonds are the natural counter-position to fossil fuel shorts; ESG capital mandates drive green bond spread compression as institutional capital has no choice but to allocate — returns benefit from both yield and spread tightening.

currencies.name: INR — Long Structural (Green Capital Inflows)
currencies.rationale: India's renewable transition attracts $30–50B/year in green FDI from Europe, Japan, and Middle East sovereign funds; this capital inflow provides structural INR support — the energy transition thesis is paradoxically INR positive over the long term.

currencies.name: NOK/EUR — Bear (Fossil Currency vs Green)
currencies.rationale: Norway's NOK is fundamentally driven by oil and gas revenues; as fossil fuel structural decline accelerates, NOK loses its commodity currency premium — EUR/NOK long captures the fossil fuel decline in the FX market.

equities.name: Coal India — Long-Dated Bear Puts
equities.rationale: Coal India is the world's largest coal miner and India's most direct fossil fuel equity; as renewable additions accelerate, Coal India's volume growth slows structurally — 12-month puts allow the annual capacity addition data to validate the thesis.

equities.name: ICLN Clean Energy ETF — Long Calls (Counter-Position)
equities.rationale: Long clean energy / short fossil fuel is the equity version of the structural displacement thesis; ICLN benefits from India's renewable buildout while Coal India suffers — holding both as a pairs strategy reduces market beta while maximizing sector rotation alpha.

title: India Banking NPA Stress Risk
desc: Rising global rates, INR depreciation, and slowing growth could re-ignite NPA stress in Indian banking. Credit event is a tail risk to hedge.
strategy: Long puts on banking sector / straddles for binary protection

commodities.name: MCX Gold — Long (Crisis Hedge)
commodities.entry: Strategic crisis hedge allocation to MCX Gold
commodities.rationale: Banking NPA stress events historically drive gold demand in India; both institutional (safe haven) and retail (loss of confidence in banks) channels bid MCX Gold — it is the most reliable domestic hedge in an India banking crisis scenario.

commodities.name: MCX Crude — Neutral (NPA Amplifier Monitor)
commodities.entry: Track crude as NPA stress amplifier
commodities.rationale: Crude oil prices directly impact NPA risk through the aviation and logistics sector credit exposure; monitoring crude levels relative to airline and transport company debt service capacity provides early warning for NPA cycle acceleration.

fixed_income.name: India HY Corporate Bonds — Bear Position
fixed_income.rationale: HY corporate bonds will reprice first in an NPA stress cycle; the current 11–13% yield doesn't adequately compensate for the credit risk in an environment where PSU banks' GNPA ratios are rising from already elevated levels.

fixed_income.name: India Gsec Short-Term — Long (Safe Haven)
fixed_income.entry: Rotate out of credit into T-bills
fixed_income.rationale: T-bills provide risk-free INR yield during NPA stress periods; the flight to safety within India's fixed income market drives Gsec yields lower (price higher) — defensive positioning while monitoring NPA data quarterly.

currencies.name: USD/INR — Long (Banking Stress = Capital Flight)
currencies.rationale: Banking NPA stress events in India historically trigger USD/INR jumps of 3–5% within weeks as FIIs sell financial sector stocks and repatriate capital; the currency trade captures the FII flow reversal that accompanies banking sector deterioration.

currencies.name: USD — Long (Global Risk-Off if Systemic)
currencies.entry: Buy UUP as tail risk hedge
currencies.rationale: If India's NPA cycle creates a systemic banking event, global risk-off ensues — USD strengthens as institutional capital exits all EM assets simultaneously; UUP provides portfolio-level protection against the tail scenario of a full NPA crisis.

equities.name: BANKBEES — Bear Put Spread
equities.entry: Buy ATM put / Sell 10% OTM put
equities.rationale: BANKBEES is the Bank Nifty ETF — the most direct equity expression of Indian banking NPA stress; puts capture sector-level selling without single-stock selection risk, particularly important in a stress scenario where the weakest links are not always obvious ex-ante.

equities.name: INDA — Bear Put Spread (Financial Sector Weight)
equities.rationale: INDA has 25%+ financial sector weighting; NPA stress events compress the entire Indian financial sector simultaneously — INDA puts provide US-listed, USD-denominated exposure to the banking stress thesis with the additional INR depreciation amplification.
